description:
**Company Description** 




 Trangile Services is a global technology firm specializing in transforming operations across retail, supply chain, logistics, and healthcare domains. By synergizing robust enterprise platforms with advancements in Data \& AI, automation, and cloud technologies, we empower businesses to optimize processes and enhance customer experiences. With over 600 professionals across key global locations, including India, Singapore, the UK, the Middle East, and the USA, Trangile delivers agile and scalable solutions. Our core services range from consulting and implementation of Oracle and Infor WMS to managed services, cloud migration, and Data \& AI solutions via the Trangile AI Lab. As an ISO\-certified company, we are committed to delivering innovation, quality, and security.
 



  





**Role Description** 




 This is a full\-time remote role for a Data Scientist. The Data Scientist will analyze complex datasets, perform statistical modeling, and develop advanced machine learning models to provide insights and actionable strategies. Responsibilities include designing and implementing data solutions, creating data visualizations, and collaborating with cross\-functional teams to address business challenges. The role also involves staying up\-to\-date with the latest data science techniques and tools to enhance decision\-making processes.
 



  





 Technical Skills \& Experience:
 



 o Experience: Minimum 3 year of experience in production\-
 



 level Data Science or Machine Learning.
 



 o Core Languages: Proficiency in Python and SQL is required.
 



 o Deep Learning (Traditional): Strong experience with
 



 frameworks like TensorFlow or PyTorch. Familiarity with
 



 architectures such as CNNs (for image) and LSTMs/GRUs
 



 (for time\-series/sequential data) is required.
 



 o Generative AI \& NLP: Practical knowledge of Transformer
 



 architectures (BERT, GPT). Experience with LLM
 



 orchestration frameworks (e.g., LangChain, Google ADK) is
 



 highly beneficial.
 



 o Reinforcement Learning: Understanding of RL concepts for
 



 decision\-making and optimization tasks is a plus.
 



 o Cloud Platform: Experience in Google Cloud Platform
 



 (GCP) is preferred, specifically BigQuery and Vertex AI.
 



 o Data Engineering: Familiarity with Vector Databases (e.g.,
 



 pgvector, Pinecone, Milvus) for semantic search and RAG
 



 implementation.
 



 o Communication: Ability to explain complex AI/ML concepts
 



 to non\-technical stakeholders and business users.
 


* o Good command of written and spoken English and Chinese

description:
**About Man Group**
 Man Group is a global alternative investment management firm focused on pursuing outperformance for sophisticated clients via our Systematic, Discretionary and Solutions offerings. Powered by talent and advanced technology, our single and multi\-manager investment strategies are underpinned by deep research and span public and private markets, across all major asset classes, with a significant focus on alternatives. Man Group takes a partnership approach to working with clients, establishing deep connections and creating tailored solutions to meet their investment goals and those of the millions of retirees and savers they represent.
   

  

 Headquartered in London, we manage $227\.6 billion\* and operate across multiple offices globally. Man Group plc is listed on the London Stock Exchange under the ticker EMG.LN and is a constituent of the FTSE 250 Index. Further information can be found at www.man.com
   

  

* As at 31 December 2025


**Job Profile Summary**
 The Quantitative Portfolio Management Analyst role spans a broad spectrum of research, investment, and technical tasks. The successful candidate for this highly competitive position will work closely with a team of Portfolio Managers and other talented Portfolio Analysts to implement the required investment strategies. This involves both validating the inputs to our models as well as the output from the quantitative signals used to make "buy/sell" decisions. The Quantitative Portfolio Management Analyst t will also monitor portfolio risk and country/sector exposures, analyse portfolio returns, and conduct analysis for client communications. ln addition to the day\-to\-day implementation of the business’ strategies, successful candidates will have the opportunity to contribute to research projects in conjunction with the central research team.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Work with Portfolio Managers to validate the inputs and outputs of our investment signals and assist with the day to day management of the portfolio
* Analyse the output of our portfolio construction process to ensure the integrity of our investment philosophy
* Work with large data sets and liaise with outside data vendors to identify data quality issues
* Design tools for evaluating portfolio implementation and performance
* Assist research teams with enhancing existing signals and creating new signals
* Conduct analysis for client communication and presentations


**Key Skills \& Requirements**
* Core Technical
+ Strong Python proficiency — you'll refactor research code into robust, tested production pipelines daily
+ Awareness of performance and optimisation — profiling, memory management, and computational efficiency when scaling research code across large universes
+ Solid software engineering practices: version control, unit testing, code review, CI/CD, and refactoring — you care about code quality, not just code that works
+ Comfortable on Linux platforms with working knowledge of shell scripting
+ Experience with data analysis libraries (NumPy, SciPy, pandas) and handling large datasets
+ Familiarity with databases and data infrastructure (SQL, data pipelines, or tools like ArcticDB)
+ Exposure to ML/DL frameworks (PyTorch, TensorFlow, scikit\-learn) or LLM\-related tooling

* Research \& Domain
+ Relevant mathematical knowledge — statistics, optimisation, or factor modeling — enough to understand the research you're productionising
+ A keen interest in financial markets and how quantitative strategies are built and deployed

* Nice\-to\-Haves
+ Prior internship in quantitative development, systematic investing, or front\-office technology

* Education
+ Currently enrolled at a top\-tier university in Computer Science, Software Engineering, Mathematics, Physics, or a related field with strong computational content


**Inclusion, Work\-Life Balance And Benefits At Man Group**
 You'll thrive in our working environment that champions equality of opportunity. Your unique perspective will contribute to our success, joining a workplace where inclusion is fundamental and deeply embedded in our culture and values. Through our external and internal initiatives, partnerships and programmes, you'll find opportunities to grow, develop your talents, and help foster an inclusive environment for all across our firm and industry. Learn more at www.man.com/diversity.
   

  

 You'll have opportunities to make a difference through our charitable and global initiatives, while advancing your career through professional development, and with flexible working arrangements available too. Like all our people, you'll receive two annual 'Mankind' days of paid leave for community volunteering.
   

  

 Our comprehensive benefits package includes competitive holiday entitlements, pension/401k, life and long\-term disability coverage, group sick pay, enhanced parental leave and long\-service leave. Depending on your location, you may also enjoy additional benefits such as private medical coverage, discounted gym membership options and pet insurance.
   

  

**Equal Employment Opportunity Policy**
 Man Group provides equal employment opportunities to all applicants and all employees without regard to race, color, creed, national origin, ancestry, religion, disability, sex, gender identity and expression, marital status, sexual orientation, military or veteran status, age or any other legally protected category or status in accordance with applicable federal, state and local laws.
   

  

 Man Group is a Disability Confident Committed employer; if you require help or information on reasonable adjustments as you apply for roles with us, please contact TalentAcquisition@man.com.

description:
**About RedotPay** 




 RedotPay is a global crypto payment fintech integrating blockchain solutions into traditional banking and finance infrastructure. Our user\-friendly crypto platform empowers millions globally to spend and send crypto assets, ensuring faster, more accessible, and inclusive financial services. RedotPay advances financial inclusion for the unbanked and supports crypto enthusiasts, driving the global adoption of secure and flexible crypto\-powered financial solutions. Join us in shaping the future of finance and making a meaningful impact on a global scale.
 



  





**Position Summary** 




 We are seeking a highly organized, proactive, and adaptable Executive Assistant. In this role, you will be more than just a scheduler; you will act as a strategic partner and gatekeeper, ensuring the executive’s time is focused on the highest\-priority initiatives. You will manage the chaos of a busy office, handle sensitive information with discretion, and act as a key liaison between the executive and internal/external stakeholders.
 



  





**Key Responsibilities** 




**Administrative \& Calendar Management** 



* Proactively manage a complex and dynamic calendar, prioritizing appointments, resolving conflicts, and ensuring the executive’s time is aligned with business goals.
* Coordinate all logistics for internal and external meetings, including room bookings, agenda preparation, technology setup (Zoom, Teams, etc.), and catering.
* Serve as the primary point of contact for the executive’s office, screening calls and emails with professionalism and discretion.



**Communication \& Correspondence** 



* Manage a high\-volume inbox: triage emails, draft responses on behalf of the executive, and flag critical items requiring immediate attention.
* Prepare, proofread, and edit correspondence, communications, presentations, and other documents.
* Draft and distribute internal communications, memos, and meeting agendas.



**Travel \& Expense Management** 



* Arrange complex domestic and international travel itineraries, including flights, accommodations, ground transportation, and visas.
* Prepare detailed travel packets and ensure the executive is prepared for all trip\-related engagements.
* Prepare and submit accurate and timely expense reports, ensuring adherence to company policy.



**Meeting \& Project Support** 



* Attend key meetings to take minutes, track action items, and follow up with stakeholders to ensure deadlines are met.
* Assist with the planning and execution of company events, all\-hands meetings, and board meetings.



**Relationship Management \& Gatekeeping** 



* Build and maintain trusting relationships with internal teams, board members, investors, and key clients.
* Act as a gatekeeper, using sound judgment to decide when to involve the executive and when to handle matters independently.



  





**Qualifications:** 



* **Experience:** 
 3\+ years of experience as an Executive Assistant supporting C\-level executives or senior leadership.
* **Technical Proficiency:** 
 Advanced proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite (Outlook, Word, Excel, PowerPoint) and/or Google Workspace. Experience with collaboration tools like Zoom.
* **Communication:** 
 Exceptional written and verbal communication skills; ability to interact professionally with all levels of an organization and external partners.
* **Organization:** 
 Unwavering attention to detail and the ability to juggle multiple priorities in a fast\-paced environment.
* **Discretion:** 
 Proven ability to handle highly confidential information with tact and integrity.

* Building, maintaining, and utilizing financial models to assess business performance, capital structures, and investment outcomes

Our Interns are exposed to Jefferies’ full range of top\-tier investment banking products and leading industry expertise by working closely with senior bankers on pitches and live deals. .

The Rights Systems team builds and operates Spotify Rights Center (SRC) — Spotify’s rights management platform that enables Spotify and its partners to identify, manage, and enforce music licensing rights across all the content available on the platform.
   

  

 Comparable in scope to YouTube’s Content ID, SRC is purpose\-built for Spotify’s ecosystem and underpins the company’s strategy for video and emerging content types. Over the past year, the team has taken SRC from concept to a fully operational production platform, delivering automated content scanning, policy management, enforcement pipelines, appeals workflows, manual claims, and analytics capabilities.
   

  

 The team has grown from a single squad to three squads (\~30 bandmates) and continues to scale as a strategic investment area for Spotify over the next several years.
   

  

 What You'll Do
   

  

* Build and maintain the data pipelines and analytics infrastructure that power Spotify’s rights management platform
* Own batch and streaming pipelines that generate core datasets used by Spotify Rights Center, including processing content segments, joining rights metadata, and producing match data that drives the product
* Develop and evolve analytics models that transform pipeline and service data into reporting for system reliability, rightsholder adoption, and business ROI
* Partner closely with product managers, backend engineers, and insights teams to define metrics, build dashboards, and generate data that informs strategic decisions around platform expansion
* Maintain data export pipelines connecting backend services and the data warehouse to ensure downstream consumers receive timely and accurate data
* Implement strong data quality practices, including validation tests, alerts, and monitoring to ensure reliable pipeline outputs
* Contribute to technical solutions that support licensing, financial engineering, and content platform stakeholders
* Participate in product ideation with engineers, researchers, product managers, and domain experts across the team
* Contribute to a collaborative engineering culture and support continuous learning through hack days, reading groups, and internal training


 Who You Are
   

  

* You have strong SQL skills and deep experience with data modeling and warehouse design
* You have experience building and operating batch data pipelines at scale using tools such as Scio, Apache Beam, Spark, or similar frameworks
* You are comfortable working with modern cloud data warehouses such as BigQuery, Snowflake, or similar technologies
* You have experience with analytics engineering tools like dbt and understand layered data modeling approaches (staging, transformation, reporting)
* You have worked with workflow orchestration platforms such as Flyte, Airflow, or similar systems
* You can write production\-quality code in languages such as Scala, Python, or Java
* You understand data quality practices and have built monitoring and alerting systems for pipeline reliability
* You take ownership of solutions end\-to\-end, from understanding business questions to deploying and validating data pipelines
* You communicate clearly with both technical and non\-technical stakeholders
* You enjoy learning new domains and tackling complex data challenges such as rights management, content matching, and multi\-territory licensing


 Where You'll Be

**What We Do**
 As a recognized leader in providing Private Markets Solutions to clients across the globe, we manage approximately $145\.6B in discretionary assets under management and $859\.8B in non\-discretionary assets under management, as of September 30, 2025\.
   

  

**The Opportunity**
 The Portfolio Management Group sits at the heart of Hamilton Lane’s portfolio journey with investors.
   

  

 We start with quantitative research to provide insights when designing private market portfolios, strategically steering them as they mature, and ensuring portfolio outcomes are achieved by placing investments and managing all factors that impact net returns for investors.
   

  

 The team partners with investment teams (who source and diligence each investment) and client managers (who manage relationships with investors) to provide robust portfolios for evergreen funds (semi\-liquids), separately managed accounts and closed\-end funds across all asset classes in private markets.
   

  

**Your Responsibilities Will Be To**
 PMG London summer interns will be given projects combined with recurring tasks to complete in the 8 week programme. The programme will conclude with a presentation you will provide to the PMG team in London.
   

  

**Projects**
 These are projects that are either quantitative research based related to private markets, or technical process enhancements that help the team speed up existing workflows
   

  

* Example of a project:
+ Landscape of European private credit, comparisons to US private credit and how investors can build better portfolios combining European private credit alongside US private credit


**Recurring Tasks**
 These are tasks that give you insight on the day\-to\-day work analysts on the team have to complete. They may relate to our workflow for portfolio proposals, quantitative research or equity portfolios
   

  

* Example of a recurring task:
+ Attend weekly pre\-investment committee meetings for buyout investments, listening to deals being presented every Thursday
+ Summarise the key points for each deal needed to make portfolio decisions to members of the PMG team. To be discussed every Friday in our weekly portfolio management meeting

 Types of work analysts do that you will be exposed to:

* Ability to work effectively within a team, able to show examples of being a team player and working towards a shared goal. For example, via group projects, societies, sports or music
* Skilled in effectively communicating insights and conclusions to diverse audiences
* Examples of being a self\-starter with a drive to excel

description:
Our 30\-year journey rides on the passion of over 27,000 seafarers and 1,000 onshore professionals. Today, we are one of the largest independent third\-party ship management companies managing over 650\+ diverse types of vessels.
 



  





 Headquartered in Hong Kong SAR, China, we operate on a global scale having 27 offices in 12 countries. Our client base spans over 100 world\-class ship owners, including Fortune 500 companies from China, Greece, India, Japan, Korea, Netherlands, Norway, Turkey and the USA, among others.
 



  





 In a shore career at FLEET, you will be working with a team of a highly passionate, self\-driven and committed group of people. We aim to be a place where you can achieve your full potential, regardless of your background. We are looking for individuals who are ambitious about making a strong contribution to FLEET’s short and long\-term sustainable growth – whether you are dealing directly with clients or working in a role supporting the business, such as technology, legal or communications.
 



  





**Job Position Summary** 




 At Fleet Management, technology is a key pillar of our strategy, and an exciting opportunity has arisen — we are looking for a detail\-oriented, delivery\-focused individual to join our team shaping the digital future of maritime excellence.
 



  





 As a Business Analyst in our Digital/IT team, you will play a hands\-on role in defining and delivering the features that power 600\+ ships and 27,000 seafarers worldwide. Your strength lies in translating customers’ needs into clear, structured user stories and acceptance criteria which are the building blocks that turn ideas into reliable, scalable solutions within the PARIS ecosystem.
 



  





 Your mission? To bring precision and clarity to every stage of product delivery using data to guide decisions and collaborating across teams to support smooth adoption. If you are driven by structured thinking, collaborative problem solving, and making a real impact through well\-crafted requirements, join us to navigate the future of maritime digitalisation together.
 



  





**Key Roles and Responsibilities** 




 As a Business Analyst, your typical day might include:
 



  





  





***Clear Navigator: Clarifying Requirements \& Product Definition:***



* Define what matters – Develop clear user stories and acceptance criteria that align with the PARIS product vision, ensuring every feature addresses a genuine user need
* Apply product thinking – Deliver workable, practical solutions whilst creating repeatable product features that scale across the fleet
* Map the detail – Define personas, process flows, and data flows to support UI and database design, ensuring features are practical and scalable across the fleet
* Validate before release – Collaborate with QA Engineers to verify that stories meet acceptance criteria and are fit for purpose ahead of every release



  





  





***Strong Team Worker: Collaboration \& Change Management:***



* Build strong partnerships – Work closely with Operational Product Owners, Development Leads, QA Engineers, and UX Designers to understand needs and deliver innovative solutions that meet and exceed expectations
* Communicate with clarity – Convey requirements, decisions, and trade\-offs clearly and concisely across stakeholders and onshore/offshore development teams
* Support smooth adoption – Support rollout strategies for new capabilities, contributing to training, communications, and stakeholder engagement to ensure smooth adoption across Fleet Management







  





***Watchkeeper: Organisation \& Thinking Clearly:***



* Let data guide decisions – Use data and metrics as inputs to provide justification and prioritisation for feature development
* Stay organised and proactive – Manage your time effectively, working independently and in line with priorities set by the team
* Contribute to improvement – Proactively highlight opportunities to improve ways of working that benefit both your own contribution and the broader team's effectiveness
* Stay current– Maintain awareness of industry best practices, Agile methodologies and tools to continuously improve your capability







  





**Relationship** 








  





***INTERNAL:***



* Operational Product Owners \& other stakeholders (Based in HK, India, Singapore, Cyprus, Philippines, China)
* Customer Experience (CX) team including Product Team, UX Designers and QA Engineers (based in HK \& India)
* Development team, Ops \& Support (based in HK \& India)







  





***EXTERNAL:***



* Interaction with Owners/Clients, Suppliers and/or third\-party solution providers







  





**Job Experience, Functional Knowledge and Qualifications** 








  





***ESSENTIAL – You MUST have all these skills and experiences 🤩***



* 3\+ years of experience working in a Business Analysis role in developing repeatable, scalable IT products
* Effective and confident communication in written and spoken English; getting messages across clearly and concisely
* Process mapping proficiency with clear thinking ability to define and document end to end process flows, data flows
* Strong mindset on understanding the use of data to help drive prioritisation; demonstrated through the use of either:
* Advanced Excel skills, or
* Use of database skills such as SQL and ElasticSearch or Google Analytics for UX analytics
* Demonstration of influencing senior stakeholders (Manager level and above)
* Strong in being able to create clearly defined user stories addressing both functional and non\-functional elements
* Applied experience in AI prompting for research, use case discovery, requirements gathering







  





***DESIRABLE – It’s great if you have these too 😇***



* Proficiency with JIRA (sprint planning, backlog refinement), Figma (design collaboration and feedback), and cross\-tool workflow coordination. Comfortable managing design\-to\-delivery handoffs
* Demonstrated experience in Agile/Scrum environments, with the flexibility to adapt across Waterfall or Hybrid methodologies depending on project context
* Qualification in process mapping (such as Lean Six Sigma)
* Maritime / Shipping industry experience would be a huge plus or similar industries such as Aviation, Logistics, or Transport







  





**Competencies** 



* Analysis \& Problem Solving: Uses critical thinking to address problems. Able to perform root cause analysis on complex problems to identify underlying trends and put forward well\-thought out solutions to address the causes.
* Listening \& Communicating: Focuses on the individual they are communicating with. Writes and expresses thoughts clearly adjusting as necessary to the audience. Asks questions to clarify.
* Collaboration, Inclusion \& Teamwork: A good team player that is personable, friendly, polite and takes the time to know people. Helps new colleagues and visitors feel welcome. Mentors and encourages junior members.
* Customer Focus: Understands the needs of the customer clarifying requirements and expectations. Adapts as necessary to changing requirements and is responsive, helpful with all requests. Sets high quality for service delivery.
* Planning \& Organising: Uses the supplied tools effectively, staying on top of routine tasks and planning for busy periods. Promptly updates people when plans change.
* Initiative: Challenges existing ways of doing things. Looking for continuous improvement without being asked. Identifies ways to improve efficiency and effectiveness in own work and others in the team.
* Accountability: Responsible for delivery own work without unnecessary supervision. Always demonstrates integrity and ethical behaviour being a role model for others. Invites constructive feedback adjusting actions accordingly. Thinks through mistakes made and finds ways to prevent reoccurrence.







  





 Fleet Management Limited is committed to diversity, equity and inclusion in the workplace. We prohibit discrimination and harassment of any kind based on race, color, sex, religion, sexual orientation, national origin, disability, genetic information, pregnancy, or any other protected characteristic as outlined by local laws.

title: Graduate program, Finance
matched_skills: Financial Modeling, Due Diligence, Data Governance, Python, SQL
gaps_in_skill: US GAAP, IFRS, Financial Statement Preparation, Tax Due Diligence, Multi-entity Consolidation, Investor Relations
description:
At Bending Spoons, we’re striving to build one of the all\-time great companies. A company that serves a huge number of customers. A company where team members grow to their full potential. A company that functions at unparalleled levels of effectiveness and efficiency. A company that creates value for shareowners at an extraordinary rate. And a company that does so while adhering to high ethical standards.
   

  

 In pursuit of this objective, we acquire and improve digital businesses, not to sell on, but to own and operate for the long term. The transformations we make are often deep—designed to speed up innovation, benefit customers, and strengthen business performance. Here, hierarchy is minimal and teams are small and talent\-dense. We operate established products with the ambition, agility, and urgency of a startup. Across the company, we integrate AI deeply into how we work so that human judgment and machine intelligence reinforce each other.
   

  

 For a talented, driven, and collaborative individual, working at Bending Spoons is an opportunity to learn, make an impact, and progress their career at an exceptionally high rate. That’s our promise to such a candidate.
   

  

 We close on acquisitions, restructure entities, and consolidate financials across jurisdictions—all at a pace that would break most finance teams. We need someone who finds that exciting, not exhausting.
   

  

 This is a graduate program that will flexibly expose you to a variety of projects, spanning from the internal financial management of the company to the integration of newly acquired businesses into our financial infrastructure. You'll work on genuinely hard problems—multi\-entity consolidations, acquisition due diligence, US GAAP and IFRS alignment—and build the technical foundations to grow rapidly into a finance professional. If you’re the kind of person who wants to learn fast, take on difficult finance problems early, and build the judgment and technical depth required to grow into an exceptional finance professional, you’ll thrive here.
   

  

**A few examples of your responsibilities** 
* **Technical accounting at real depth.** 
 Prepare and own financial statements under US GAAP, with IFRS alignment where required. Exercise judgment on revenue recognition, consolidation, and intercompany flows—in a context where the reporting framework evolves constantly as we acquire and integrate new businesses.
* **Financial planning and control.** 
 Produce reporting that actually drives decisions, collaborate with cross\-functional teams on budget management, and ensure fiscal policies hold up as the business scales.
* **Due diligence and post\-M\&A integration.** 
 Support financial and tax due diligence for acquisitions and related restructuring activities. Contribute to post\-M\&A integration, ensuring smooth financial, operational, and reporting alignment.
* **Investor relations and fundraising support.** 
 Help prepare board meetings and minutes. Own financing to support M\&A, engaging with banks, investors, and institutional stakeholders.
* **Equity plans.** 
 Manage and optimize equity plans—implement, support team members on an ongoing basis, and evolve the program as it grows.


**What we look for** 
* **Reasoning ability.** 
 Given the necessary knowledge, you can solve complex problems. You think from first principles, and structure your ideas sharply. You resist the influence of biases. You identify and take care of the details that matter.
* **Drive.** 
 You’re extremely ambitious in everything you do—and your initiative, effort, and tenacity match the intensity of your ambition. You feel deeply responsible for your work. You hold yourself to a high—and rising—bar.
* **Team spirit.** 
 You give generously and without the expectation of receiving in return. You support the best idea, not your idea. You're always happy to get your hands dirty to help your team. You’re reliable, honest, and transparent.
* **Proficiency in English.** 
 You read, write, and speak proficiently in English.


**What we offer** 
* **Incredibly talented, entrepreneurial teams.** 
 You’ll work in small, result\-oriented, autonomous teams alongside some of the brightest people in your field.
* **An exceptional opportunity for growth.** 
 We go to great lengths to hire individuals of outstanding potential—then, our priority is to put them in the ideal position to thrive. Spooners in their 20s lead products worth hundreds of millions of dollars. And if you’ve got what it takes, you’ll soon be playing an essential role in major projects, too.
* **Competitive pay and access to equity in the company.** 
 Typically, we offer individuals at the start of their career an annual salary of £85,797 in London and €66,065 elsewhere in Europe. For a candidate that we assess as possessing considerable relevant experience, the salary on offer tends to be between £112,189 and £250,512 in London, and €107,837 and €188,848 elsewhere in Europe. Compensation varies by location and expected impact, and grows rapidly as you gain experience and translate it into greater contributions. For individuals who demonstrate exceptional capability, we may offer compensation that extends beyond the usual ranges to reflect their higher expected impact. If you're offered a permanent contract, you'll also be able receive some of your pay in company equity at a discounted price, thus participating in the value creation we achieve together. If relocating to Italy, you may enjoy a 50% tax cut.
* **A flexible start date and part\-time options.** 
 You don't need to wait until graduation to apply. We offer flexible start dates and the possibility to begin part\-time, transitioning to full\-time as you complete your degree. Many Spooners joined before graduating and progressively took on greater responsibility, with arrangements that allowed them to do so without compromising their education.
* **All. These. Benefits.** 
 Flexible hours, remote working, unlimited backing for learning and training, top\-of\-the\-market health insurance, a rich relocation package, generous parental support, and a yearly retreat to a stunning location. We help each Spooner set up the conditions to do their best work.


**Commitment \& contract**
 Fixed\-term or internship. Full\-time or part\-time.
   

  

**Location**
 Milan (Italy), London (UK), Madrid (Spain), Warsaw (Poland) or remote in selected countries.
   

  

**The selection process**
 In our screening process, we prioritize verifiable signals of excellence, regardless of seniority. Some people hold back because they feel they lack experience or have an “imperfect” CV. If you like the role and believe you could excel over time, don’t self\-reject.
   

  

 If you pass our screening, you’ll be asked to complete one or more tests. They are challenging, may involve unfamiliar problems, and can take several hours.
   

  

 We set the bar high and won’t extend an offer until we’re confident we’ve found the right candidate. This is why a job may remain open for months or be reposted several times.
   

  

 We consider all applicants for employment and provide reasonable accommodations for individuals with disabilities—please let us know through this form.
   

  

**Before you apply**
 If you’ve applied before but didn't receive an offer, we recommend waiting at least one year before applying again.
   

  

 Bending Spoons is a demanding environment. We’re extremely ambitious and we hold ourselves—and one another—to a high standard. While this tends to lead to extraordinary learning, achievement, and career growth, it also requires significant commitment.
   

  

 To help you ramp up quickly and set yourself up for success, we recommend spending your first few months working from our Milan office, regardless of your long\-term work location. It’s the best way to rapidly absorb our company culture and build trust with your new teammates. We’ll support you with generous travel and accommodation assistance. After that, you’re welcome to work from our offices in Milan or London, or remotely from approved countries—depending on what we agree at the offer stage.
   

  

 If the role speaks to you and you’re excited to give your best, we’d love to hear from you. Apply now—we can’t wait to meet you.

description:
**About This Role**
 BlackRock is one of the world’s preeminent asset management firms and a premier provider of global investment management, risk management and advisory services to institutional, intermediary and individual investors around the world. BlackRock offers a range of solutions — from rigorous fundamental and quantitative active management approaches aimed at maximizing outperformance to highly efficient indexing strategies designed to gain broad exposure to the world’s capital markets. Our clients can access our investment solutions through a variety of product structures, including individual and institutional separate accounts, mutual funds and other pooled investment vehicles, and the industry\-leading iShares® ETFs.
   

  

**Business Unit Overview**
 The International Cash Management Group manages a suite of products that includes Prime and Government Money Market and Ultra\-Short Duration Mutual funds and Separate Accounts. BlackRock’s cash management style is designed to meet each client’s objectives of capital preservation, liquidity and yield – in that order. This philosophy is built on a risk\-controlled framework, featuring careful analysis of credit, interest rate trends, and relative value opportunities which ultimately produce investment returns on a risk\-adjusted basis. Our disciplined approach to investing originated in 1973 when we pioneered the first constant\-dollar money market fund dedicated to institutional investors.
   

  

**Job Purpose/Background/Responsibilities**
 This role sits within the International Cash Portfolio Management Team and is primarily a junior portfolio management / trader role based in Singapore or Hong Kong. The successful candidate will spend a large part of the day as a dealer supporting the international cash investment process by investing in money market instruments (Time Deposits, Reverse Repo, Commercial Paper, Certificates of Deposit, short Government bonds and Bills, Supranationals and any other suitable instruments), with an emphasis on major Asian currencies. The person will also play a major role in collating data and using technology to help improve desk processes and analysis of portfolios and positions. A natural curiosity and drive will help the successful candidate establish themselves within a busy and proactive team, helping research markets, regulation, credit, technology and ESG.
   

  

* To execute daily trading of money market instruments in support of the Lead Portfolio Managers with a focus on Time Deposits and government bills
* To understand and contribute to the strategy and construction of HKD / USD and other Asian currency money market Funds as well as separately managed accounts
* To produce analytics and portfolio level data for strategy, client and other key meetings
* To build and maintain strong professional relationships with external trading counterparties (brokers and dealer banks) and other parts of the broader ecosystem (rating agencies, etc.)
* To build strong partnerships with internal stakeholders such as credit, product strategy, risk, operations, pricing, legal \& compliance, and contribute to our diverse and inclusive work environment
* To work with other data and technology focused colleagues to help create greater operational efficiencies across the desk


**Knowledge / Experience Required**
* Ideally 3\-5 years of work experience in cash/short\-duration fixed income portfolio management or trading, and transferable skills to benefit this role.
* An enthusiastic attitude with a willingness to learn
* Ability to multi\-task and prioritize tasks in a demanding and fast\-paced environment
* Foundational knowledge of financial markets and understanding of credit and risk management
* An interest in developing research, quantitative or analytical skills, particularly using data systems (Knowledge of Aladdin a plus)
* Knowledge and understanding of digital assets and tokenised real\-world assets preferred
* Experience with Python, SQL, Tableau, Bloomberg, VBA a plus
* An interest in developing presentation and written communication skills
* Interpersonal skills to help foster an inclusive culture of teamwork and knowledge sharing
* Fluent in English. Cantonese or Mandarin preferred but not mandatory.


**Our Benefits**
 To help you stay energized, engaged and inspired, we offer a wide range of benefits including a strong retirement plan, tuition reimbursement, comprehensive healthcare, support for working parents and Flexible Time Off (FTO) so you can relax, recharge and be there for the people you care about.
   

  

**Our hybrid work model**
 BlackRock’s hybrid work model is designed to enable a culture of collaboration and apprenticeship that enriches the experience of our employees, while supporting flexibility for all. Employees are currently required to work at least 4 days in the office per week, with the flexibility to work from home 1 day a week. Some business groups may require more time in the office due to their roles and responsibilities. We remain focused on increasing the impactful moments that arise when we work together in person – aligned with our commitment to performance and innovation. As a new joiner, you can count on this hybrid model to accelerate your learning and onboarding experience here at BlackRock.
   

  

**About BlackRock**
 At BlackRock, we are all connected by one mission: to help more and more people experience financial well\-being. Our clients, and the people they serve, are saving for retirement, paying for their children’s educations, buying homes and starting businesses. Their investments also help to strengthen the global economy: support businesses small and large; finance infrastructure projects that connect and power cities; and facilitate innovations that drive progress.
   

  

 This mission would not be possible without our smartest investment – the one we make in our employees. It’s why we’re dedicated to creating an environment where our colleagues feel welcomed, valued and supported with networks, benefits and development opportunities to help them thrive.
   

  

 For additional information on BlackRock, please visit @blackrock \| Twitter: @blackrock \| LinkedIn: www.linkedin.com/company/blackrock
   

  

 BlackRock is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We evaluate qualified applicants without regard to age, disability, family status, gender identity, race, religion, sex, sexual orientation and other protected attributes at law.

Marking our unlisted assets to market would provide S$35 billion of value uplift and bring our mark to market net portfolio value to S$469 billion. 
 




  
 




 Our Purpose “So Every Generation Prospers” guides us to make a difference for today’s and future generations. 
 




  
 




 Operating on commercial principles, we seek to deliver sustainable returns over the long term. 
 




  
 




 We have 13 offices in 9 countries around the world: Beijing, Hanoi, Mumbai, Shanghai, Shenzhen, and Singapore in Asia; and Brussels, London, Mexico City, New York, Paris, San Francisco, and Washington, DC outside Asia.  
 




  
 




 For more information on Temasek, please visit www.temasek.com.sg.
   



 For Temasek Review 2025, please visit www.temasekreview.com.sg.
   



 For Sustainability Report 2025, please visit https://www.temasek.com.sg/content/dam/temasek\-corporate/sustainability/2025/Temasek\-Sustainability\-Report\-2025\.pdf.
 




  
 



**Introduction** 


  
 




 Temasek's Cybersecurity department is looking to hire full\-time interns to support various cybersecurity initiatives across different teams. This internship will give you an opportunity to gain first\-hand experience in planning and executing cybersecurity projects while developing valuable technical and business skills in a global investment company.
 

  
 





  
 

  
 





 The department comprises of 4 key technical pillars: (1\) Defence and Resilience, (2\) Governance and Oversight, (3\) Architecture and Engineering Operations, and (4\) Innovation, Incubation \& Initiatives. Specific domain requirements are listed below, but interns would have the opportunity for cross\-functional learning and experience.
 

  
 




**1\.Defence and Resilience Intern** 


  
 



  
 


* This role will assist in supporting the uplift of Temasek’s cybersecurity resilience through development and execution of an effective cyber resilience programme and establishment of new/advanced cyber capabilities to strengthen cyber awareness, enhance cyber readiness and monitoring capabilities. This enables us to driving a culture of cyber resilience across Temasek and its ecosystem.
* Drive security awareness through innovative and engaging methods, creating a culture where cybersecurity becomes second nature
* Develop compelling technical content for
 

 both technical and non\-technical audiences
* Design interactive training programs that transform complex security concepts into actionable behaviours
* Plan and
 

 facilitate realistic tabletop exercises that prepare operational and management teams for real\-world 
 

 cyber
 

  threats
* Monitor effectiveness of existing awareness programmes gather feedback, and recommend enhancements for continuous improvement



**2\. Governance and Oversight Intern** 


  
 


* This role will assist in supporting continuous enhancement and implementation of an effective governance and risk management framework to manage technology, data security, and cyber risks across the enterprise. This will enable Temasek to be better prepared to mitigate and manage these risks in the face of evolving cybersecurity threats.
* Perform Governance, Risk and Compliance related duties including 
 

 policy reviews, risk assessments, and control testing
* Identify gaps in governance frameworks and propose enhancements to strengthen oversight
* Provide support to GRC related reviews and processes
* Track audit findings and 
 

 follow up with IT partners/vendors on audit/assurance findings
* Maintain accurate records of compliance processes risk registers, and assurance reports



**3\. Architecture and Engineering Operations Intern (Security Operations)** 


  
 


* This role will assist in supporting Temasek’s security operations through monitoring and compliance checks, streamlining documentation, coordinating incident response, enhancing governance to address operational risks, and providing project management support for ongoing initiatives.
* Perform ongoing monitoring on security operations hygiene, controls compliance by leveraging variety of management tools and techniques to detect operational irregularities and lapses.
* Support development, maintenance and streamline documentation of operational SOPs, playbooks, configuration baselines, operational reports to improve operational efficiency and effectiveness.
* Collaborate with cross\-functional teams to ensure prompt and efficient incident response and support containment activities.
* Drive and improve security governance to identify operational gaps and risk across the security solution stack.
* Provide project management support in ongoing initiatives


**4\. Architecture and Engineering Operations Intern (Architecture)** 

  
 


* This role will assist in supporting in the development and execution of Temasek’s cybersecurity defense strategy and planning for future cybersecurity needs.
* Support security technology evaluations and proof\-of\-concept
 

 for security solutions, documenting findings and recommendations
* Research emerging security threats, technologies, 
 

 vulnerabilities, and industry trends
* Assist in creating technical security documentation, diagrams and presentations 
 

 to support solution design and stakeholder communication



**5\. Innovation, Incubation \& Initiatives Intern** 


  
 


* Assist the team by researching and evaluating emerging technology risks (e.g., quantum computing, agentic AI) and identifying potential security use\-cases and mitigations.
* Support initiatives to enhance detection of hard\-to\-detect threats by exploring advanced solutions (e.g., automation, advanced analytics) to improve alert quality, triage speed, and mitigation workflows.
* Additional scope may include supporting the establishment of an Insider Risk \& Security (IRS) capability to improve visibility of insider threats and enable timely response actions.



**Requirements** 


  
 


* Able to commit full\-time for a minimum of 20 weeks, preferably from July 2026 onwards, and will still be enrolled with a recognised university by the end of the internship.
* Please indicate your availability (start and end date) and expected graduation date.
* University undergraduates pursuing any of these majors/degrees: Computer Science, Information Security, Information Systems, Cybersecurity, IT Management.
* We also welcome applicants from other disciplines with demonstrated interest in cybersecurity.



  
 




**What we are looking for**





  
 


* Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
* Prior internship experience(s) with demonstrated capabilities in cybersecurity, IT security or related fields will be considered a strong plus.
* Adaptive in fast paced environment and able to multi\-task and handle complex projects with multiple stakeholders.
* Actively listen to instructions and demonstrate an understanding of assignment expectations (deadlines and deliverables).
* Demonstrate critical thinking, curiosity, resourcefulness, and an eagerness to learn.
* Meticulous and detail\-oriented with the ability to identify, manage, and resolve issues.
* Comfortable with ambiguity, self\-motivated, and proactive.
* Possess integrity and ability to maintain confidentiality of company\-sensitive information.
* Highly proficient in Microsoft Office 365 Suite such as Outlook, Teams, SharePoint, PowerPoint, Excel, and Word.

description:
**Our vision is to transform how the world uses information to enrich life for all.**
 Join an inclusive team passionate about one thing: using their expertise in the relentless pursuit of innovation for customers and partners. The solutions we build help make everything from virtual reality experiences to breakthroughs in neural networks possible. We do it all while committing to integrity, sustainability, and giving back to our communities. Because doing so can fuel the very innovation we are pursuing.
   

  

**Aim Of The Project**
 This internship offers the opportunity to contribute to cutting‑edge research within Micron’s Technology Development (TD) organization. The Yield Enhancement (YE) Lab is equipped with state\-of\-the\-art Transmission Electron Microscopes (TEMs) and Focused Ion Beam (FIB) systems. The project focuses on advancing three‑dimensional (3D) Electron Tomography techniques to enable nanoscale structural and compositional characterization for emerging memory technologies.
   

  

 As an intern, you will collaborate with process engineers, integration teams, and TEM specialists to develop innovative TEM characterization workflows. Your contributions will help accelerate the development of next‑generation memory products.
   

  

**Project Scope**
**Learning Opportunities:**
* Hands\-on experience preparing 3D NAND samples using Focused Ion Beam (FIB) techniques.
* Execution of 3D Electron Tomography experiments using TEM/STEM/EFTEM.
* Exposure to tilt\-series acquisition, 3D reconstruction, and interpretation of tomographic data.
* Direct collaboration with cross\-functional engineering teams (process integration, device engineering, reliability).
* Experience documenting technical findings and presenting results in professional engineering settings.
* Insight into real\-world applications of advanced microscopy in semiconductor R\&D.


**Deliverable**
**Impact of Project:**
* Development and optimization of 3D tomography workflows for next\-generation memory nodes.
* High\-quality tomographic datasets and reconstructed 3D models that enhance device understanding.
* Technical reports summarizing methodology, findings, and recommendations for adoption into TD workflows.


**Skillset Required**
* Currently pursuing a PhD in Materials Science and Engineering, Applied Physics, or a related discipline.
* Basic understanding of semiconductor device physics and materials characterization.
* Hands\-on experience with TEM systems (e.g., Nion, TFS, JEOL).
* Familiarity with data analysis tools such as DigitalMicrograph, MATLAB, or Python.
* Experience with 3D reconstruction software (Avizo, IMOD, TomoJ, Inspect3D) is a plus.
* Strong analytical and problem\-solving skills; demonstrated innovation is highly valued.
* Effective communication skills and a collaborative mindset.

 Materials Science \& Engineering, Applied Physics, Nanotechnology, Electron Microscopy, Semiconductor Engineering

description:
**About This Role**
 BlackRock is one of the world’s preeminent asset management firms and a premier provider of global investment management, risk management and advisory services to institutional, intermediary and individual investors around the world. BlackRock offers a range of solutions — from rigorous fundamental and quantitative active management approaches aimed at maximizing outperformance to highly efficient indexing strategies designed to gain broad exposure to the world’s capital markets. Our clients can access our investment solutions through a variety of product structures, including individual and institutional separate accounts, mutual funds and other pooled investment vehicles, and the industry\-leading iShares® ETFs.
   

  

**Business Unit Overview**
 The International Cash Management Group manages a suite of products that includes Prime and Government Money Market and Ultra\-Short Duration Mutual funds and Separate Accounts. BlackRock’s cash management style is designed to meet each client’s objectives of capital preservation, liquidity and yield – in that order. This philosophy is built on a risk\-controlled framework, featuring careful analysis of credit, interest rate trends, and relative value opportunities which ultimately produce investment returns on a risk\-adjusted basis. Our disciplined approach to investing originated in 1973 when we pioneered the first constant\-dollar money market fund dedicated to institutional investors.
   

  

**Job Purpose/Background/Responsibilities**
 This role sits within the International Cash Portfolio Management Team and is primarily a junior portfolio management / trader role based in Singapore or Hong Kong. The successful candidate will spend a large part of the day as a dealer supporting the international cash investment process by investing in money market instruments (Time Deposits, Reverse Repo, Commercial Paper, Certificates of Deposit, short Government bonds and Bills, Supranationals and any other suitable instruments), with an emphasis on major Asian currencies. The person will also play a major role in collating data and using technology to help improve desk processes and analysis of portfolios and positions. A natural curiosity and drive will help the successful candidate establish themselves within a busy and proactive team, helping research markets, regulation, credit, technology and ESG.
   

  

* To execute daily trading of money market instruments in support of the Lead Portfolio Managers with a focus on Time Deposits and government bills
* To understand and contribute to the strategy and construction of HKD / USD and other Asian currency money market Funds as well as separately managed accounts
* To produce analytics and portfolio level data for strategy, client and other key meetings
* To build and maintain strong professional relationships with external trading counterparties (brokers and dealer banks) and other parts of the broader ecosystem (rating agencies, etc.)
* To build strong partnerships with internal stakeholders such as credit, product strategy, risk, operations, pricing, legal \& compliance, and contribute to our diverse and inclusive work environment
* To work with other data and technology focused colleagues to help create greater operational efficiencies across the desk


**Knowledge / Experience Required**
* Ideally 3\-5 years of work experience in cash/short\-duration fixed income portfolio management or trading, and transferable skills to benefit this role.
* An enthusiastic attitude with a willingness to learn
* Ability to multi\-task and prioritize tasks in a demanding and fast\-paced environment
* Foundational knowledge of financial markets and understanding of credit and risk management
* An interest in developing research, quantitative or analytical skills, particularly using data systems (Knowledge of Aladdin a plus)
* Knowledge and understanding of digital assets and tokenised real\-world assets preferred
* Experience with Python, SQL, Tableau, Bloomberg, VBA a plus
* An interest in developing presentation and written communication skills
* Interpersonal skills to help foster an inclusive culture of teamwork and knowledge sharing
* Fluent in English. Cantonese or Mandarin preferred but not mandatory.


**Our Benefits**
 To help you stay energized, engaged and inspired, we offer a wide range of benefits including a strong retirement plan, tuition reimbursement, comprehensive healthcare, support for working parents and Flexible Time Off (FTO) so you can relax, recharge and be there for the people you care about.
   

  

**Our hybrid work model**
 BlackRock’s hybrid work model is designed to enable a culture of collaboration and apprenticeship that enriches the experience of our employees, while supporting flexibility for all. Employees are currently required to work at least 4 days in the office per week, with the flexibility to work from home 1 day a week. Some business groups may require more time in the office due to their roles and responsibilities. We remain focused on increasing the impactful moments that arise when we work together in person – aligned with our commitment to performance and innovation. As a new joiner, you can count on this hybrid model to accelerate your learning and onboarding experience here at BlackRock.
   

  

**About BlackRock**
 At BlackRock, we are all connected by one mission: to help more and more people experience financial well\-being. Our clients, and the people they serve, are saving for retirement, paying for their children’s educations, buying homes and starting businesses. Their investments also help to strengthen the global economy: support businesses small and large; finance infrastructure projects that connect and power cities; and facilitate innovations that drive progress.
   

  

 This mission would not be possible without our smartest investment – the one we make in our employees. It’s why we’re dedicated to creating an environment where our colleagues feel welcomed, valued and supported with networks, benefits and development opportunities to help them thrive.
   

  

 For additional information on BlackRock, please visit @blackrock \| Twitter: @blackrock \| LinkedIn: www.linkedin.com/company/blackrock
   

  

 BlackRock is proud to be an Equal Opportunity Employer. We evaluate qualified applicants without regard to age, disability, family status, gender identity, race, religion, sex, sexual orientation and other protected attributes at law.

 THE FUNDING STRATEGIST TEAM

All streams require close collaboration with the trading desks and business stakeholders.

Our team members have a wide variety of quantitative, academic and cultural backgrounds. This diversity helps us to find innovative solutions for our complex business problems.

* Strong communication skills. Ability to drive outcomes quickly in multi\-constrained and ambiguous business context.

description:
**About Us** 




 Longbridge is an AI\-Powered online brokerage that serves global investors with optimal trading experiences through building worldwide trading infrastructure and networks.
 



 Established in March 2019, Longbridge holds 22 financial licenses and qualifications across the United States, Singapore, Hong Kong, New Zealand and other regions, having secured over US $150 million in strategic investments from major financial and investment institutions. Its subsidiary, Longbridge Securities (Hong Kong) Limited (CE No.: BPX066\), holds Type 1, 2, 4, and 9 licenses issued by the Hong Kong Securities and Futures Commission (SFC).
 



  





**Role Overview** 




 You will be responsible for the end\-to\-end research, selection, and management of global fund products (including Mutual Funds, Private Equity, and ETFs). You will build robust research frameworks to evaluate fund managers, investment strategies, and underlying assets to provide actionable investment recommendations. This role bridges the gap between complex market data and client\-centric wealth solutions.
 



  





**Key Responsibilities** 



* **Fund Research \& Strategy:** 
 Build and maintain a comprehensive global fund research system. Conduct deep\-dive due diligence on fund managers, investment philosophies, and risk\-return profiles to produce institutional\-grade research reports.
* **Portfolio Construction \& Product Curation:** 
 Select high\-quality funds and manage the firm’s product list. Align product offerings with the company’s wealth management strategy and diverse client risk profiles to optimize portfolio structures.
* **Sales Enablement \& Investor Education:** 
 Lead the creation of investment education (InvEd) content and marketing collateral. Partner with relationship managers to provide technical product support, conduct training sessions, and enhance both team and client conviction in our product suite.
* **Market Intelligence \& Risk Management:** 
 Monitor global market trends, regulatory changes, and product innovations. Continuously refine selection criteria and research methodologies to mitigate risks and ensure full compliance.
* **Lifecycle Management \& Operations:** 
 Coordinate with Risk, Compliance, and Operations teams to streamline the entire product lifecycle—from initial onboarding and shelf\-launch to ongoing post\-investment monitoring.
* **Client\-Centric Optimization:** 
 Act as the feedback loop between front\-line business teams and the product department. Refine the research service system based on client needs to improve product\-suitability and drive AUM growth.



  





  





**Qualifications \& Requirements** 



* Bachelor’s degree or higher in Finance, Economics, Statistics, or Investment. Candidates with international education or overseas work experience are highly preferred.
* 3 years relevant experience in fund research, product selection, or investment analysis. Prior experience in a securities brokerage, wealth management firm, or asset management house is a significant advantage.
* Deep understanding of global fund investment strategies and risk assessment methodologies.Proven track record of producing independent, high\-quality research.
* Exceptional logical reasoning, and a results\-driven mindset. You should be a collaborative team player with a sharp sensitivity to market movements.
* Familiarity with Hong Kong financial regulations (SFC) is required. Professional designations such as CFA, FRM, or HKSI licensing is a plus.

 29th June 2026
   

  

**End Date:** 
 27th August 2026
   

  

**Location:** 
 Canary Wharf, London
   

  

**Duration:** 
 9 weeks (full\-time)
   

  

**Availability Requirement:** 
 Candidates must be available for the entire internship period.
   

  

**About The Role**
 We are looking for motivated and enthusiastic undergraduate students to join us for a 9\-week summer internship. This is a fantastic opportunity to gain hands\-on experience in a dynamic and collaborative environment while exploring the world of Trade Credit Insurance.
   

  

 As a Summer Intern, you will be part of a small group of interns and work closely with one of our key departments:
 **Credit** 
 or
 **Commercial** 
 . You’ll contribute to meaningful projects, develop new skills, and gain practical experience in your assigned area.

****Key Responsibilities****
**Depending on your assigned department, your responsibilities may include:**
**Credit Function**
* Supporting the assessment and monitoring of credit risk for our clients.
* Assisting in the analysis of financial data and evaluating the creditworthiness of businesses.
* Collaborating with the team to prepare reports and recommendations for decision\-making.
* Gaining exposure to credit risk management tools and processes.


**Commercial Function**
* Supporting the development and management of client relationships.
* Assisting in the preparation of proposals, presentations, and marketing materials.
* Conducting market research and competitor analysis.
* Gaining insights into sales strategies and customer engagement processes.


**What You’ll Gain**
* Practical, hands\-on experience in a professional environment.
* Exposure to the Trade Credit Insurance industry and its key functions.
* Opportunities to network with professionals and fellow interns.
* A deeper understanding of how different departments collaborate to deliver value to clients.


**What We’re Looking For**
 We are seeking candidates who are:
   

  

* Currently enrolled in undergraduate studies (ideally in Business, Finance, Economics, or a related field).
* Curious and eager to learn about Trade Credit Insurance and the financial services industry.
* Strong communicators with excellent interpersonal skills.
* Analytical thinkers with attention to detail and problem\-solving abilities.
* Team players who are proactive and adaptable in a fast\-paced environment.


**For Credit Function Internships**
* Strong proficiency in Microsoft Office Suite, particularly advanced knowledge of Excel, Access, and VBA for Excel.
* Experience with Power BI or other data visualization tools is a plus.
* Ideally, experience in data processing, management, and analysis.
* Familiarity with financial analysis and credit systems is highly desirable.


**Additional Requirement**
 Due to the short duration of the internship, candidates must already have the right to work in the UK. Unfortunately, visa sponsorship is not available for this role.
   

  

**Why Join Us?**
 At Allianz, we are dedicated to fostering a culture of learning, collaboration, and innovation. This internship is designed to provide you with a meaningful and rewarding experience that will help you develop your skills and prepare for your future career.
   

  

**Application Process**
 To apply, please submit your CV along with a brief cover letter explaining:
   

  

* Your interest in the internship.
* Your preferred department (Credit or Commercial).


**Application Deadline:** 
 27/04/2026
   

  

 We look forward to welcoming you to our team this summer!
   

  

 We are seeking candidates who are naturally curious and have a keen interest in technology and innovation. As we navigate the future, we value individuals who are eager to stay updated with the latest digital trends and advancements. The ideal candidate should demonstrate a proactive approach to learning and adapting to new tools and technologies, especially
 **Artificial Intelligence (AI) powered tools** 
 , showcasing a forward\-thinking mindset that aligns with our vision for growth and progress.
   

  

 \#United Kingdom
   

  

 \#NEUR
   

  

 Allianz Group is one of the most trusted insurance and asset management companies in the world. Caring for our employees, their ambitions, dreams and challenges, is what makes us a unique employer. Together we can build an environment where everyone feels empowered and has the confidence to explore, to grow and to shape a better future for our customers and the world around us. Join us. Let’s care for tomorrow.
   

  

 Allianz Trade is the trademark used to designate a range of services provided by Euler Hermes. We are the global leader in trade credit insurance and a recognized specialist in the areas of surety, collections, structured trade credit and political risk. Headquartered in Paris, we are present in more than 50 countries with 5,500 employees. In 2021, our global business transactions represented 931 billion Euro in exposure.
   

  

 We at Allianz believe in a strong inclusive culture that encourages people to speak their minds, get involved and question the status quo. We are proud to be an equal opportunity employer and encourage you to bring your whole self to work, no matter where you are from, what you look like, who you love, or what you believe in. We therefore welcome applications regardless of race, ethnicity or cultural background, age, gender, nationality, religion, social class, disability, sexual orientation, or any other characteristics protected under applicable local laws and regulations. Great to have you on board. Let's care for tomorrow.

title: Data Engineer

description:
**About BTSE**
 BTSE Group is a global leader in fintech and blockchain technology, anchored by three core business pillars: Exchange, Payments, and Infrastructure Development. Serving over 100 corporate clients worldwide, we provide white\-label exchange and payment solutions. Our offerings encompass everything from exchange infrastructure hosting and development to custody, wallets, payments, blockchain integration, trading, and more. We are looking for talented professionals in marketing, operations, customer support, and other departments. The roles offered may be on\-site, remote, or hybrid, in collaboration with our local partner.
   

  

**About The Opportunity**
 We're building an AI\-powered research platform for institutional investors. Our platform turns vast amounts of market, alternative, and proprietary data into actionable intelligence — powered by AI agents that depend on clean, reliable, real\-time data to do their job.
   

  

 We need someone to own data. Not manage a team that does data. Own it — from finding the right sources, to getting them flowing, to making sure they stay healthy at scale.
   

  

 Today we ingest from hundreds of sources. That number is growing fast. The sources are diverse: real\-time market feeds, regulatory filings, on\-chain blockchain data, news, social sentiment, alternative datasets, and proprietary client data. Some are free APIs. Some are $10K/month enterprise contracts. Some are clients pushing their own data into our platform. Every one of them is different, and most of them will break in ways you don't expect.
   

  

 You'll evaluate vendors, negotiate deals, build integrations, monitor quality, track costs, and make the call on what's worth paying for. When something breaks at 2 AM, you'll know why before the alert finishes firing.
   

  

 This is an end\-to\-end ownership role. No handoffs.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Build and maintain integrations with a large and growing number of external data sources — APIs, WebSockets, file drops, streams, scrapers, and formats you haven't seen yet
* Evaluate and compare data vendors across quality, reliability, coverage, cost, and terms of service
* Negotiate contracts and manage commercial relationships with data providers
* Design and operate high\-throughput ingestion pipelines handling mixed workloads (real\-time, near\-real\-time, batch, event\-driven)
* Build monitoring that tells you — before anyone else — when data is late, wrong, incomplete, or drifting
* Manage data quality at scale: anomaly detection, cross\-source validation, schema drift detection, gap filling
* Handle both structured data (time\-series, tabular) and unstructured data (documents, text, images) with appropriate extraction and storage
* Track costs per source, usage per consumer, and ROI — recommend what to keep, upgrade, or cancel
* Build tooling that makes adding the next data source faster than the last one
* Use AI tools aggressively in your daily work — for code generation, testing, documentation, anomaly analysis, and anything else that makes you faster


**Requirements**
 \*\*You've done this before:\*\*
   

  

* 5\+ years building data pipelines that run in production, 24/7, with real SLAs
* Deep hands\-on experience with SQL databases and time\-series data
* Python as your primary language, comfortable with async programming
* You've integrated with dozens of external APIs and dealt with the reality of unreliable vendors, changing schemas, rate limits, and bad documentation
* You've built monitoring and alerting for data systems — not as an afterthought but as part of how you work


**You Think About The Whole Picture**
* You don't just connect to an API. You think about what happens when it goes down, when the schema changes, when the data is wrong, when the bill doubles
* You understand that data has a cost and a value, and not every source is worth keeping
* You've worked with data vendors commercially — contracts, pricing tiers, usage negotiations


**You Use AI Daily**
* AI coding tools are part of your workflow today, not something you're curious about
* You can articulate specifically how AI makes you faster and where it doesn't help
* You'd be frustrated if you couldn't use AI in your work


**Nice to have**
* Experience with financial or crypto market data
* Experience with streaming systems (Kafka or similar) at scale
* Vector database or embedding pipeline experience
* Experience with unstructured data extraction (PDFs, documents, NLP)


**Perks \& Benefits**
* Senior individual contributor role with full ownership of the data domain
* Direct access to leadership — no bureaucracy, fast decisions
* AI tools provided and encouraged across all work
* Remote\-friendly, async\-first
* Compensation commensurate with experience


 We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

title: Data Analyst
matched_skills: SQL, Tableau, Data Analysis, Business Intelligence, Data Modeling, Financial Analysis, Strategic Business Reporting, Project Coordination (XFN), Fast-paced Environment Adaptation
description:
Job Overview
 



 We are seeking a data\-driven professional with extensive hands\-on experience in big data analytics to join our team. The ideal candidate will be responsible for transforming complex datasets into clear, actionable insights that drive strategic business decisions. This role requires strong technical proficiency in data analytics tools, programming languages, and visualization platforms, along with the ability to manage multiple projects in a fast\-paced environment. The candidate should demonstrate exceptional problem\-solving skills, analytical thinking, and the ability to deliver accurate, insightful reports that support business growth and operational efficiency.
 







 Job Responsibilities
 



 Based on business goals and business models, establish a comprehensive, accurate, and objective indicator system and monitoring and analysis framework. At the same time, based on the business monitoring and analysis framework, clearly understand the overall business situation and locate business issues;
 



 Through thematic analysis, conduct in\-depth analysis of business issues, provide data support and decision\-making basis for company operational decisions, marketing strategies, product directions, etc.;
 



 Precipitation analysis method and monitoring framework, extract data product requirements, collaborate with relevant teams (such as development team, product team) and promote the landing of data products;
 



 Collaborate with relevant teams to organize training and sharing of concepts, skills, methods, and tools related to data technology.
 







 Job Requirements
 



 Bachelor's degree or above in statistics, mathematics, information technology, or economics, with 3\-5 years of relevant work experience;
 



 Experience in digital currency, internet, and e\-commerce is preferred;
 



 Good logical analysis ability and data sensitivity, able to extract core results from massive data, timely discover and analyze deep changes and root causes behind business appearances;
 



 Proficient in data extraction tools such as SQL, proficient in operating tools such as Excel, PPT, Tableau, etc.;
 



 Strong logical thinking ability, outstanding language expression ability, proficient in independently writing business Data Analysis reports;
 



 Good business knowledge and understanding of business mechanisms and models, able to insight into business opportunities from the perspective of business development;
 



 Outstanding communication and teamwork skills, with certain XFN and cross\-team project experience, able to independently promote project execution.
 



 Fluent English skills
 



 Strong adaptability and time management skills to thrive in a fast\-paced work environment
 



  





 工作职责
 



 1、根据业务目标和业务模式，建立一套全面、准确、客观的指标体系和监测分析框架。同时，基于这一业务监测分析框架，能够清晰了解整体业务状况，并定位业务问题
 



 2、通过专题分析，对业务问题进行深入剖析，为公司运营决策、营销策略、产品方向等方面提供数据支持和决策依据
 



 3、降水分析方法与监测框架，提取数据产品需求，与相关团队（如开发团队、产品团队）协作，并推动数据产品的落地
 



 4、与相关团队协作，组织培训并分享与数据技术相关的概念、技能、方法及工具。
 







 职位要求
 



 1、拥有统计学、数学、信息技术或经济学专业学士学位或以上学位，并具有 3 至 5 年的相关工作经验
 



 2、具备数字货币、互联网及电子商务方面的经验者优先考虑
 



 3、具备良好的逻辑分析能力和数据敏感性，能够从海量数据中提炼核心结果，及时发现并分析表象背后的深层变化和根本原因
