* Proficiency in Microsoft Excel and PowerPoint, with the ability to produce clear, accurate analysis and professional presentation materials.
* Strong written and verbal communication skills, coupled with a high level of attention to detail and organization.
* Ability to manage multiple priorities in a fast‑paced, deadline‑driven environment, with a collaborative mindset and eagerness to learn.



  





**Community \& Engagement, and Reasonable Accommodations** 




 At Jefferies, we have made a commitment to build a culture that provides opportunities for all employees regardless of our differences, and supporting a workforce that is reflective of the communities where we work and live. As a result, we are able to pool our collective insights and intelligence to provide fresh and innovative thinking for our clients.
 



  





 Jefferies is an equal employment opportunity workplace. We practice equal opportunity for all, regardless of race, religion, ancestry, color, gender, pregnancy, age, physical or mental disability or medical condition, national origin,
 
 marital, family and social status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, genetic information, religion, trade union membership or any other factor protected by law. description:
Binance is a leading global blockchain ecosystem behind the world’s largest cryptocurrency exchange by trading volume and registered users. We are trusted by 300\+ million people in 100\+ countries for our industry\-leading security, user fund transparency, trading engine speed, deep liquidity, and an unmatched portfolio of digital\-asset products. Binance offerings range from trading and finance to education, research, payments, institutional services, Web3 features, and more. We leverage the power of digital assets and blockchain to build an inclusive financial ecosystem to advance the freedom of money and improve financial access for people around the world.
   

  

**About Binance Accelerator Program**
 Binance Accelerator Program is a concise fixed\-term program designed for Early Career Talent to have an immersive experience in the rapidly expanding Web3 space. You will be given the opportunity to experience life at Binance and understand what goes on behind the scenes of the worlds’ leading blockchain ecosystem. Alongside your job, there will also be a focus on networking and development, which will expand your professional network and build transferable skills to propel you forward in your career. Learn about BAP Program HERE
   

  

**Who May Apply**
 Current university students and recent graduates
   

  

**About The Role**
 You'll work directly on the AI systems powering Binance AI Products and next\-generation agentic trading features — alongside the full\-time algorithm team, on real production challenges.
   

  

 You own deliverables, run experiments, and ship code that matters. You build components of AI systems (agents, pipelines, evaluation tools), debug real systems, and work with engineers to ship features that reach users.
   

  

 This is not a "watch and learn" program. You are expected to build, contribute, and ship.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Contribute to the design and development of LLM\-powered pipelines for agentic trading — including reasoning agent components, tool\-use frameworks via MCP, and automated workflow execution across crypto markets.
* Build and evaluate prompt engineering strategies, test\-time scaling approaches, and retrieval architectures for crypto\-native data sources — on\-chain data, market feeds, news, and sentiment signals.
* Design and run model evaluation experiments — defining quality metrics for agent reasoning in financial contexts, executing benchmarks, and synthesizing results into actionable findings.
* Analyze agent performance characteristics in live trading scenarios — covering decision accuracy, latency, reliability, and adversarial robustness.
* Apply AI\-native development practices using agentic coding tools as a standard part of the engineering workflow — writing and executing Python code for strategy logic, data pipelines, and agent evaluation.


**Requirements**
* Currently pursuing a Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Data Science, Statistics, Mathematics, or related technical field.
* Expected graduation in 2026 or 2027\.
* Strong Python programming skills with demonstrated AI\-native development practices — you use agentic coding tools (Claude Code, Cursor, GitHub Copilot Workspace) as a core part of your workflow.
* Foundational understanding of how large language models work — attention mechanisms, prompting, and the difference between standard generation and reasoning models.
* Structured problem\-solving approach and ability to operate independently on defined tasks.


 Why Binance
   

  

* Shape the future with the world’s leading blockchain ecosystem
* Collaborate with world\-class talent in a user\-centric global organization with a flat structure
* Tackle unique, fast\-paced projects with autonomy in an innovative environment
* Thrive in a results\-driven workplace with opportunities for career growth and continuous learning
* Competitive salary and company benefits
* Work\-from\-home arrangement (the arrangement may vary depending on the work nature of the business team)


 Binance is committed to being an equal opportunity employer. We believe that having a diverse workforce is fundamental to our success.
   

  

*By submitting a job application, you confirm that you have read and agree to our Candidate Privacy Notice .*
 We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

description:
**ROLE OVERVIEW** 




 This role is a senior role focused on supporting business growth by using intelligence, strategy, and commercial knowledge. This person finds growth opportunities by turning market information, audience data, and competitor insights into clear recommendations that help the business and clients succeed.
 



 This role goes beyond traditional insights work by actively searching for growth areas, understanding where opportunities exist, and giving teams the information and tools they need to make quick decisions. They need to work closely with growth, strategy, and client teams across APAC, needing experience in different markets and the ability to turn complex data into easy\-to\-understand business stories.
 



  





**KEY RESPONSIBILITIES** 




**1\. Strategic Insight Development** 



* Conduct research across market trends, customer behaviour, competitive activity, and internal performance metrics to inform strategic priorities and business decisions.
* Analyse and synthesise complex data sets into clear, actionable insights that support presentations, pitches, strategic planning, and commercial decision\-making.
* Create insight\-driven content — including reports, briefs, and presentations — that inform growth initiatives, client opportunities, and business direction.
* Maintain a strong understanding of industry dynamics across APAC markets to proactively surface emerging opportunities and risks.
* Lead insight workstreams end\-to\-end, from scoping and methodology design through to synthesis and final delivery.



**2\. Advisory \& Consultative Support** 



* Act as a strategic advisor to teams by providing evidence‑based recommendations and frameworks for story telling or decision‑making.
* Partner with growth, strategy, and client teams to ensure insights are embedded into planning, pitches, and client conversations.
* Facilitate insight discussions and workshops to help teams interpret data, align on implications, and prioritize actions.
* Support on client projects and engagements, including but not limited to workshop development and delivery.
* Communicate findings to stakeholders with clarity, confidence, and narrative structure that drives alignment and action



**3\. Stakeholder Management** 



* Build and maintain strong relationships with senior internal stakeholders across functions including growth, strategy, product, and leadership.
* Manage external client relationships to deliver insight\-related projects
* Navigate complex stakeholder environments across multiple APAC markets, adapting communication and approach to local context and audience needs.
* Collaborate with cross\-regional teams to ensure consistency of insight quality and approach across markets.



**4\.** 
**Business Enablement and Opportunity Intelligence** 



* Identify and surface commercial growth opportunities through proactive analysis of market conditions, category trends, audience behaviour, and competitive landscape across APAC.
* Provide intelligence and solutioning support to growth and commercial teams, translating insight into clear opportunity frameworks that drive revenue and business development.
* Partner with internal teams to develop insight\-led propositions and narratives that unlock new client opportunities or expand existing relationships.
* Contribute to pitch and proposal development by injecting relevant insight, data, and growth intelligence that strengthens the commercial case.



**Mandatory Requirements** 




**Education** 



* Bachelor's degree (minimum) in Business, Economics, Marketing, Social Sciences, Data Science, or a related discipline.
* Postgraduate qualification (MBA or Master's) is advantageous but not essential.



**Experience** 



* 3 years minimum of experience, ideally 5 years in insights, strategy, management consulting, or a related analytical field.
* Proven cross\-market experience across multiple APAC markets (e.g. Singapore, Australia, Southeast Asia, China, Japan), with the ability to contextualise insights within regional nuances.
* Demonstrated experience managing both internal and external stakeholders at a senior level, including clients, leadership teams, and cross\-functional partners.
* Track record of delivering high\-quality, insight\-led work that has directly influenced business strategy or client outcomes.
* Experience designing and delivering client workshops, presentations, and advisory engagements.



**Skills \& Competencies** 



* Strong quantitative and qualitative analytical capabilities, with proficiency in data interpretation and synthesis.
* Exceptional written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to craft compelling narratives for diverse audiences.
* Advanced proficiency in PowerPoint and data visualisation tools; familiarity with research platforms and analytics tools is an advantage.
* Highly structured thinker with the ability to break down complex problems into clear frameworks and recommendations.
* Commercially astute with a strong understanding of business dynamics and client needs.
* Collaborative and adaptable, with the confidence to influence and challenge constructively at all levels.



**Key Performance Indicators:** 



* Insight quality and strategic impact
* Contribution to strategic initiatives and client engagements

This involves both validating the inputs to our models as well as the output from the quantitative signals used to make "buy/sell" decisions. The Quantitative Portfolio Management Analyst t will also monitor portfolio risk and country/sector exposures, analyse portfolio returns, and conduct analysis for client communications. ln addition to the day\-to\-day implementation of the business’ strategies, successful candidates will have the opportunity to contribute to research projects in conjunction with the central research team.

**About Our Roles**
**Investment Banking Operations:** 
 Investment Banking Operations is responsible for all operational functions for Investment Banking. We support all product flows and provide effective control for the firm’s Investment Banking strategy
   

  

 We provide full lifecycle deal support across the Financing, Lending, and Advisory businesses. The Investment Banking Operations team is comprised of subject matter experts in onboarding, deal origination, structuring, execution, and servicing in addition to adherence to market and regulatory policies and standards. The Information Services Group manages and sources market data and research to facilitate deal solicitation, client service, and ongoing market analysis.
   

  

**Principal Responsibilities Of The Syndicate Execution Team Are**
* ECM/DCM Syndicate execution support across APEJ markets – IPOs, block trades, convertible bonds
* Working on ECM/DCM transactions from pre\-launch through to completion of all post pricing requirements
* Close coordination with other Syndicate banks to manage the bookbuilding and allocation process
* Ensuring MNPI is handled sensitively according to the Compliance \& Regulatory framework
* Ad hoc reports and analysis – regulatory and for the IB team


**Job Responsibilities**
* You will sit on the ECM/DCM Syndicate desk in HK supporting all facets of APEJ execution
* Ensure all required postings \& underwriting approvals are in place prior to transaction launch
* System set\-up and deal creation using the Dealogic bookbuilding platform to facilitate execution
* You will manage the bookbuilding process including reviewing all orders via Sales into Dealogic
* You will release client allocations and handle any deal related queries from Sales during a transaction
* Work on all aspects post\-pricing to ensure all regulatory and risk requirements are completed on time
* Liaise with Controllers, ECM/DCM \& IB to ensure PnL is accurately recorded \& all Syndicate fees are settled
* Work with League Table Agencies to ensure all GS transactions are credited accurately
* Provide ad hoc deal reporting , investor analysis \& league table stats to senior ECM/DCM \& Sales leadership
* Complete a range of daily \& weekly ECM/DCM reporting on behalf of the Syndicate desk
* Work with the IB Ops teams globally to provide colour around the execution of APEJ transactions

 The Ideal candidate for this position will have the following qualifications:
   

  

* Strong communication skills, able to clearly articulate complex ideas
* Strong relationship skills with the ability to build networks
* Strong analytical and organisational skills
* Strong Excel skills
* Effective team player
* Self starter, able to work towards targets with minimal supervision
* Commercial awareness, able to understand the impact of the role on client experience and firm profitability
* Ability to drive projects and programmes across the team
* Mandarin / Cantonese language skills preferred

description:
**Green Court Capital Management** 
 (GCCM) is a private, employee\-controlled asset manager with offices in Hong Kong, Shanghai and Singapore, managing Greater China Equity mandates totaling approximately US$5 billion in assets under management. The firm employs a fundamentally driven, bottom\-up investment process with a strong culture built on integrity, partnership and long\-term results. Consistent and rigorous application of our investment philosophy has attracted a high quality global client base, including pension plans, endowments and foundations, sovereign funds and other world\-class allocators of capital. This culture, coupled with our flat organizational structure, makes it an ideal place to work for entrepreneurial, self\-motivated individuals.
 



  





 We are seeking a professional to join the firm’s Business Development \& Client Service (“BDCS”) Function. The BDCS Function has overall responsibility for the formulation and execution of GCCM’s client strategy, encompassing global capital raising, marketing to institutional investors, product planning \& development and investor relations. Select interaction with existing and prospective institutional investors is expected.
 



  





 Applicants should possess the following qualifications and attributes:
 





  





**Qualification \& Experience** 



* 2 to 5 years of relevant experience in institutional investor relations, business development, capital introduction, investment banking, or equity research/sales, preferably at a premier asset management firm, bulge bracket investment bank, or top\-tier investment consultancy
* Familiarity with Greater China capital markets, the institutional investor landscape, or China\-focused investment strategies is highly advantageous
* Exceptional written and verbal communication skills in English are required
* Bachelor’s degree in Finance, Economics, or a related field with an excellent academic background
* Active progression or completion of CFA or CAIA designation is preferred



  





**Technical \& Professional Skills** 



* Demonstrated knowledge of and genuine intellectual curiosity about capital markets, investment strategies, and macroeconomic trends
* Proven experience with investor due diligence processes, including the preparation of DDQs, RFIs, and RFPs
* Proficient in Microsoft Word, Excel and PowerPoint with the ability to prepare and construct aesthetic, bespoke investor presentations and other marketing collateral
* Familiarity with various capital market and CRM applications, including Bloomberg, Wind, and Dynamo



  





**Work Style \& Personal Attributes** 



* Self\-motivated, resourceful and accountable, able to multi\-task and focus on delivering results in a fast\-paced, deadline\-driven environment
* Meticulous and detail\-oriented, with strong organizational skills and a commitment to accuracy in all client\-facing work
* Collaborative team player with strong interpersonal skills and the ability to engage effectively with stakeholders at all levels
* Exercises sound judgment and maintains the highest standards of professional integrity, discretion, and confidentiality



  





**Given our company’s flat culture, we look for individuals who have the flexibility and aptitude to spend part of their time working in areas outside of their functional expertise. We feel this develops a better understanding of end\-to\-end processes, as well as enhancing cross\-functional teamwork.** 




  





**The interview process is quite extensive whereby selected candidates are expected to meet with many of the Partners prior to receiving an invitation to join the firm.**

 The preceding job description has been designed to indicate the general nature and level of the work performed by the employee. It is not designed to contain or be interpreted as the comprehensive inventory of all duties, responsibilities and qualifications required of employees assigned to this job.
 



  





**IMPORTANT:** 
 By submitting your information, you explicitly consent to our processing of your personal information as set out in the Terms and Conditions of Use on our website (https://www.greencourtcap.com/terms\-conditions.html), in particular Section 6 Personal Information Protection Statement.

**About Hasbro**
 Hasbro is a leading games, IP and toy company whose mission is to create joy and community through the magic of play. With 165 years of expertise, Hasbro delivers groundbreaking play experiences and reaches more than 1 billion fans annually around the world, through physical and digital games, video games, toys, licensed consumer products, location\-based entertainment, film, TV and more.
   

  

 Through its franchise\-first approach, Hasbro unlocks value from both new and legacy IP, including MAGIC: THE GATHERING, DUNGEONS \& DRAGONS, MONOPOLY, HASBRO GAMES, NERF, TRANSFORMERS, PLAY\-DOH and PEPPA PIG, as well as premier partner brands. Powered by its portfolio of thousands of iconic marks and a diversified network of partners and subsidiary studios, Hasbro brings fans together wherever they are, from tabletop to screen.
   

  

 For more than a decade, Hasbro has been consistently recognized for its corporate citizenship, including being named one of the 100 Best Corporate Citizens by 3BL Media, a 2025 JUST Capital Industry Leader, one of the 50 Most Community\-Minded Companies in the U.S. by the Civic 50, and a Brand that Matters by Fast Company. For more information, visit https://corporate.hasbro.com or @Hasbro on LinkedIn.
   

  

**The Internship Experience** 

 Hasbro’s Summer Internship Program offers a behind\-the\-scenes look at how a global play and entertainment company operates\- paired with the opportunity to make a real impact.
   

  

 Interns are fully embedded on their teams and trusted to own meaningful business projects aligned to team priorities. Throughout the summer, you’ll contribute to work that matters while also being part of a broader global intern cohort, gaining exposure to professional development sessions, networking opportunities, and leadership engagement designed to support your growth and learning.
   

  

  

  

**What You Will Do As a Sourcing Intern**
* Support the execution of global sourcing strategies for assigned product categories
* Assist with supplier research, evaluation, and onboarding, including data gathering and documentation
* Help track supplier performance related to cost, quality, delivery, and compliance
* Support cost analysis, productivity initiatives, and value‑engineering efforts
* Collaborate with cross‑functional partners including Engineering, Quality, Finance, and Project Management
* Maintain sourcing documentation, reports, and data accuracy in internal systems
* Assist with identifying sourcing risks and escalating issues as appropriate
* Support continuous improvement initiatives across the sourcing and supply network


**What You’ll Bring**
* Current undergraduate or graduate student (Spring 2026 graduates not eligible)
* Available to work full‑time
* At least one year of study in Supply Chain, Engineering, Business, Operations, or a related field
* Strong communication, organization, and time management skills
* Intermediate skills in Excel, Word, Outlook, and web‑based tools
* Interest in global manufacturing, sourcing, and consumer product development
* Passion for collaboration, play, and consumer product development


**Program Details** 

* Interview Timeline: February – April
* Summer Internship Program Dates:
* Start: Late June (target June 22\)
* End: Late August (target August 21\)
* Commitment: Full\-time (40 hours/week)
* Location: Hong Kong


**Why Hasbro** 

 Hasbro internships are designed as a launchpad for early career talent. Interns are given meaningful responsibility, exposure to senior leaders, and opportunities to explore long\-term career paths within the company. Strong performance may lead to consideration for return or full\-time roles.

**About The Role**
 Are you ready to make a positive and impactful difference in people's lives? Join AIA, a world\-class finance and insurance company, as a Customer Service Ambassador at our Wealth Select Centre \& Customer Service Centre in Hong Kong.
   

  

**Responsibilities**
* Serve customers and agents by addressing their enquiries, requests, complaints, and MCV verifications.
* Follow operation procedures in compliance with documented procedures, service benchmarks, regulatory requirements, and company policies and practices.
* Provide prompt and accurate services, policy information, and professional advice to customers.
* Manage and resolve customer problems and elevated customer complaints.
* Handle special requests relating to policy matters.
* Collaborate with Premium Collection and Underwriting departments to provide one\-stop MCV verification service.
* Support case monitoring and investigation.
* Provide quality services to customers and continuously identify areas for improvement.
* Collect customer feedback and report operation problems to the Centre Head.
* Contribute new ideas to enhance customer experience and work efficiency.
* Coach and mentor team members, and prepare training material.
* Support working partners and ensure smooth operation and service alignment.
* Perform other assigned responsibilities and duties.


**Requirements**
* University/College graduate with 4\-5 years' relevant experience OR Form Five plus at least 7\-8 years' working experience.
* Solid knowledge of life insurance.
* Excellent communication and interpersonal skills.
* Passionate about providing excellent customer service and driven by personal motivation.
* Open to changes and challenges.
* Proficient in both spoken and written English and Chinese, including Mandarin.
* Relevant license required for regulated activities.
